channel.title: Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU EN
channel.description: Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU EN RSS

item.title: Lawyers welcome vacancies for Portuguese judges
item.description:
The Macao SAR opened a competition to recruit two judges in Portugal for the first instance courts, in the civil area. Lawyers interviewed by the newspaper TRIBUNA DE MACAU applaud the decision, highlighting the importance of the presence of these professionals, due to the Portuguese matrix of Law in Macau. Pointing to the “lack of judges” existing in the territory, the lawyers also mention that magistrates coming from Portugal end up passing on their experience to young people who are graduating.
Vítor Rebelo*
Macau is expected to receive two Portuguese judges in the near future, as the Superior Council of the Judiciary of Portugal announced the recruitment of two magistrates to serve in the first instance courts in the territory, in the civil area. Lawyers in the Macao SAR welcome the arrival of two Portuguese judges, highlighting the importance of their presence for the maintenance of Portuguese law. Lawyer Icília Berenguel began by telling the TRIBUNA DE MACAU newspaper that “judges are always welcome”. "Justice wants to be faster and the more judges are working, obviously the faster justice is served. Having two Portuguese magistrates is very good, even to maintain what Macau has been. Macau has always had the presence of Portuguese-speaking judiciary and it is always good", he commented. Asked whether there is a need to bring judges to other instances, Icília Berenguel pointed out that Macau's culture, procedural rules and decisions can be practiced in one of the official languages, adding that “although we have many Chinese-speaking judges who also work a lot with Portuguese, it is more practical to have a Portuguese-speaking judge, as they will certainly work more with the Portuguese language”. “But we are in a country where the ‘one country, two systems’ framework has to be seen in the best light, so I think this cooperation is always very welcome in all areas of Law”, continued Icília Berenguel. In the same vein, Pedro Leal, a lawyer practicing in Macau for almost 40 years, is “extremely happy” with the possibility of the area of ​​justice in the territory having two more judges coming from Portugal. “For around 15 years I have been saying that it is necessary to recruit more judges, and the way to recruit them is in Portugal”, he told this newspaper, noting that “this does not mean that I am underestimating local judges, who are not better or worse than those who come from Portugal”. “The issue is that there is a lack of judges and it is impossible to train judges in record time”, he stated, noting that there are courses that are being launched, “I think every three, or four years, a judge finishes his law course, goes on to do a judicial course and then is placed on an internship”. Therefore, “you need time to adapt to the circumstances, to adapt to the judiciary, whether Judicial or Public Prosecutor's Office, and, to start dispatching cases, it takes 10 years”, he maintains. In the lawyer's opinion, “the Portuguese judges who have come to Macau have great quality and, therefore, when they arrive, they not only deliver quickly, but also teach others”. Pedro Leal recalls that the law that exists in the territory has a Portuguese origin, “which is why there is every interest in Portuguese judges coming here”, reiterating that “it has nothing to do with Portuguese and Chinese”, but rather “purely and simply with the fact that Portuguese judges have quality and everyone benefits from that, particularly in civil matters”. The lawyer recognizes that in crime “things are going well, without major delays”, while in civil matters “there are actually very long delays and, therefore, it is necessary to adapt to these circumstances”. Rui Cunha, one of the oldest lawyers practicing in Macau, also says he is “in favor” of the arrival of judges from Portugal. “I think that the presence of Portuguese magistrates brings a great history, a great experience and can help a lot in solidifying the edifice of the judicial system here in Macau”, he stated. In Macau since 1981, brought by gaming magnate Stanley Ho, Rui Cunha considers that Portuguese judges “are passing on their experience to these young people who in Macau are already qualified judges, but the experience of people with a more solid past is always favorable”. The lawyer states that it is “useful” for Portuguese judges to remain in the territory, “since all Macau Law still has roots very close to Portuguese Law and, therefore, it is always useful to maintain this connection”. As the competition is aimed at two civil judges, this sector is, for the lawyer, “the most critical part, the one that requires greater elaboration, more time for consideration, an area that requires greater exercise of intelligence, following principles and things, so it is good that they come to the civil court”.
Missing judges in the Second Instance On the other hand, lawyer Pedro Leal considers that there has also been a lack of judges in the Second Instance. “That is, there were several changes in the judiciary, some left and others went to other positions and what happens is that the processes are delayed”, he says, adding that this happens because “there is a lack of judges”. The lawyer claims to have never noticed this “resistance” to recruiting judges. “It’s always been like this in Macau.” Portugal and Macau maintain a judicial cooperation agreement that ensures the continuity of Portuguese magistrates (judges and prosecutors) in the territory, supporting the Portuguese-based legal system. This collaboration focuses on recruitment, training and the exchange of judicial experiences. The only judge currently working in the Macao SAR from Portugal is Jerónimo Alberto Gonçalves Santos, at the Court of Second Instance, after judge Rui Ribeiro brought forward the end of the special commission, which ended in May 2026, to the end of October 2025. In 2024, the Superior Council of the Judiciary rejected Carlos Carvalho's stay in the Court of First Instance. He had been in Macau for 16 years and had been invited by the territory's Independent Commission for the Appointment of Judges to continue for another two years. Asked whether or not it continues to be attractive for Portuguese judges to come and practice in the territory, Pedro Leal responded affirmatively. “I think so, it continues to be quite attractive, since a judge in Portugal earns relatively less than he earns in Macau”. On the other hand, he observed, “there are middle-aged judges from the First Instance, who come here and like to come to work in Macau, feeling attracted to the East”, being convinced that, “if competitions are opened, there will be several applications”. Icília Berenguel stressed that interest will depend, among other aspects, on the current conditions in Portugal, but considers that “it will be attractive because it is a challenging experience”.
Seniority must be between seven and 15 years According to the published notice, judicial magistrates with a seniority of more than seven years and less than 15 years can apply, with the appointment being valid for two years, possibly renewable, upon new appointment by the Independent Commission Responsible for the Appointment of Judges of the Macao SAR and approval by the Chief Executive. Applications can be submitted until March 31, and according to the document prepared by the Independent Commission Responsible for the Appointment of Judges of the Macao SAR, the selected magistrates will have a salary and benefits equivalent to those of local judges of the same category and seniority. The salary corresponds to a percentage of the Chief Executive's monthly salary, currently set at 268,297 patacas. The percentage varies depending on the magistrate's length of service, with time spent in Portugal also being taken into account. For example, judges with seven years of service receive 50% of the Chief Executive's salary; aged 11, 54%; and at 15 years old, 57%. The envisaged benefits include holiday and Christmas bonuses, the right to accommodation (work house or subsidy for rent and equipment), medical assistance for you and your family and 22 working days of holiday, to be taken during judicial holiday periods. The selection of judges is the responsibility of the Independent Commission of the Macao SAR, based on curriculum analysis and interviews with candidates considered most suitable. The Commission guarantees confidentiality regarding the data submitted by candidates.
*With Catarina Pereira

item.title: A century of panchões remembered in more than 400 artefacts
item.description:
More than 400 articles related to the history of Macau's panchão industry are on display at Galeria Sands until the end of August. Divided into six themes, the manuscripts, panchão-making tools and packaging, among other pieces, tell the centuries-old history of one of the most important traditional industries in Macau's past.
PEDRO MILHEIRÃO Sands Gallery opened yesterday the exhibition “A Radiant Century of the Iec Long Panchões Factory – An Exhibition on the Sound History and Aesthetic Memory of Macau Panchões”, which will be on view until August 31st. The exhibition brings together more than 400 artefacts, such as manuscripts, receipts, panchão-making tools, labels and packaging, which illustrate a century of growth and decline of one of the most notable industries of the 20th century in Macau. Spread across six thematic chapters, the exhibition “interweaves the collective memory and artistic evolution” of Macau’s panchão industry, says Sands China. The journey begins with an immersive prologue and extends through the stories of the industry, the mastery of artisans, historical archives, interactive experiences and the aesthetic evolution of packaging designs. The journey, which “highlights both the aesthetic value of the industry and the practical realities of production, operations, transport and export”, was curated by Ung Vai Meng, professor at the Macau University of Science and Technology (UCTM), co-organizer of the exhibition. Through an “innovative artistic transformation”, the exhibition reinterprets the cultural memory of the panchão industry, “rooted in the ‘Made in Macau’ spirit”, in “participatory art experiences” designed for the public. It is also the first exhibition that comprehensively retraces, investigates and presents the development of the panchão industry in the territory, according to the operator. Before starting the guided tour, Ung recalled that “the production of panchões was once one of the most important traditional industries in Macau”. "For many of the older residents, it represents a collective memory of the last century. In the 1950s and 1960s, panchões produced in Macau represented between around 30% and half of world production", which gave the territory a privileged position in the international market, he said. In turn, the vice-president of Sands China, Wilfred Wong, stressed that the concessionaire “has always been committed to promoting the development of Macau's diverse arts and cultures”. “Since taking on the revitalization of the Iec Long Panchão Factory in 2023, we have been actively investing resources to breathe new life into this important era in Macau’s industrial history,” he highlighted. “By collaborating with UCTM, the Macau Museum, and the Macau Archives to provide historical exhibitions and academic support, and by inviting Professor Ung Vai Meng as curator, we took advantage of the synergy of entrepreneurship and academia to present the history of panchões in its full and most authentic form”, said Wilfred Wong. Over the years, Ung Vai Meng, who was president of the Cultural Institute, has made efforts to promote and preserve Macau's cultural heritage, including participating in the drafting of the Cultural Heritage Protection Law and contributing to the restoration of the former Iec Long Panchão Factory. He donated to the UCTM Library all the articles related to the Macau panchões that he had collected over more than thirty years, and which are currently on display. Ung began his artistic apprenticeship under the guidance of painter Kam Cheong Ling. He studied drawing and watercolor and, in 1991, traveled to Lisbon to study at the “Ar.Co” art school. He holds a Master of Fine Arts from the Guangzhou Academy, a Doctor of Fine Arts from the Hangzhou Academy of Arts and was the first director of the Macau Museum of Art. In addition to this exhibition, two more exhibitions will be co-organized, by Sands and UCTM. The first, which is located at the university, focuses on “Historical Resonance: The Art of Panchão Labels in Eastern Guangdong” and will be on view until the 31st of this month. Between April 10th and May 31st, the UCTM library will host the exhibition “Timeless Treasures: Archive Materials from the Panchão Industry of Macau”. All exhibitions are free to enter. Furthermore, between 10am and 12pm tomorrow, the UCTM amphitheater will host the colloquium “Historical Memories of the Taipa Panchões Factory”. The launch ceremony was attended by Maria Helena de Senna Fernandes, director of Tourism Services, and Cheang Kai Meng, vice-president of the Cultural Institute, among other guests.

item.title: Guangdong studies Shenzhen-Zhuhai-Macao fast train
item.description:
Guangdong authorities have revealed a plan to build a Shenzhen-Zhuhai (Macau) fast train line, in addition to the already known Guangdong-Zhuhai (Macau) connection. Meanwhile, the National Development and Reform Commission stated that it will comprehensively study the construction of the “Shenzhen-Zhuhai Corridor” in the preparation of the country's 15th Five-Year Plan
The Guangdong government recently launched a competition to study the impacts of the construction of the Shenzhen-Zhuhai (Macau) and Guangzhou-Zhuhai (Macao) fast train lines in the SAR and on regional development, as well as the feasibility of building fast train stations in the central area of ​​Zhuhai. The tender suggests that there could be a fast train line between Shenzhen and Zhuhai, also covering the Macao SAR. On the other hand, in January this year, an announcement published by the Zhuhai Rail Transport Services Bureau revealed the idea of ​​building the Guangzhou-Zhuhai (Macau) line with stations in the central area of ​​Zhuhai. According to the tender document, Zhuhai's positioning as the main railway modal center on the west coast of the Pearl River and a national-level integrated transport modal center is defined. In this process, it is necessary to strengthen connections between Zhuhai and Macau in terms of transport. The objectives are to create fast train lines that allow journeys of one hour in the Greater Bay Area, three hours between Zhuhai and neighboring provincial capitals, six hours with the main cities along the Yangtze River, and nine hours with Beijing and Tianjin, the document also provides. So far, the location for the future Hengqin rapid train station has been chosen, which will also serve as a modal center with three basement floors. At this location, spaces will be reserved for the extension of the Macau Light Metro to this station and the adoption of the “one inspection” border crossing model. According to predictions from the Zhuhai authorities, after the construction of the Guangzhou-Zhuhai (Macau) line, with stations in the North of Guangzhou, Baiyun Airport in Guangzhou, Nansha, South of Zhongshan, Hezhou in Zhuhai and the Deep Cooperation Zone, a one-hour direct journey between Guangzhou and Macau will be implemented, which will mark the official connection of the MSAR to the national high-speed rail network. According to forecasts, at the end of this year, the section between Hezhou and Hengqin of this line, measuring around 23 kilometers, will meet the conditions for construction work to begin, with the project potentially requiring a total investment of 12.6 billion renminbi. On the other hand, in a response sent in reaction to a proposal presented by the deputy in the National People's Assembly Xie Jian, during the “Two Sessions” of 2026, the National Development and Reform Commission (CNDR) recognized the “important reference value” of the deputy's suggestion to “accelerate the planning and construction of the 'Shenzhen-Zhuhai Corridor'” for the review and improvement of the country's 15th Five-Year Plan proposal. According to the response released by the Shenzhen Transport Services Bureau, the CNDR stated that it will, together with the relevant bodies, comprehensively study the construction of the “Shenzhen-Zhuhai Corridor” in drawing up specific plans for integrated transport and the development of the railway network, in the 15th Five-Year Plan. Remember that the “Shenzhen-Zhuhai Corridor” aims to enable a direct 30-minute journey between the two cities. If the project is completed, it will be the first transoceanic access route in the Greater Bay Area, comprising a road and rail network. On the other hand, a member of the Transit Advisory Council suggested optimizing support facilities at Light Rail stations, to allow passengers a “friendly and convenient” travel experience. Kuok Meng Chit, also deputy director of the Chinese New Youth Association, also defended “tailored” commercial models at each station and a specific support plan to encourage young local entrepreneurs to bring new products and services to Metro stations, in order to boost the commercial atmosphere and, in the long term, alleviate financial pressures on Metro operations.

item.title: Chief demands broad collection of opinions in the preparation of the 3rd Five-Year Plan
item.description:
At a working meeting on the preparation of the Macao SAR's 3rd Five-Year Plan, the Chief Executive highlighted the duty to actively and closely coordinate with the national 15th Five-Year Plan. Furthermore, he emphasized another duty to broadly collect public opinion and encourage the participation of the entire society in the preparation of the plan, covering the period between 2026 and 2030.
The Chief Executive, Sam Hou Fai, convened and chaired yesterday a working meeting to prepare the Macao SAR's 3rd Five-Year Socio-Economic Development Plan (2026-2030), held at the Government Headquarters. According to a statement, Sam Hou Fai emphasized the duty of the 3rd Five-Year Plan to be actively and closely linked to the national 15th Five-Year Plan, based on the reality of Macau. Stressing that this task is the priority for this year, he stressed that all guardianships must cooperate closely and coordinate to successfully complete all related work. At the same time, the leader of the Macao SAR highlighted the duty to promote dissemination and interpretation, broadly collect public opinion and encourage the participation of the entire society, to reach consensus. The meeting was attended by the president of the Legislative Assembly, André Cheong, the president of the Court of Final Appeal, Song Man Lei, and all the holders of the main positions. At the meeting, the Chief Executive noted that the preparation of the 3rd Five-Year Plan is proceeding in an orderly manner, with all departments having provided relevant information. As he explained, the preliminary version is being prepared, including the document for public consultation, having carried out a series of intensive preliminary studies and research and proactively consulting the opinions of different sectors. Sam Hou Fai urged all guardianships to speed up coordination to ensure the progress of drafting. Based on the spirit of the “Two Sessions”, namely the content referred to in the 15th Five-Year Plan, Sam Hou Fai presented three demands on the preparation and implementation of the 3rd Five-Year Plan of the Macao SAR. First, he urged proactive and close coordination with the national 15th Five-Year Plan, deeply implementing the new tasks and requirements defined therein. In addition to demanding a complete understanding of the strategic positioning and development theme defined by the 15th Five-Year Plan, in particular the contents relating to Macau, Sam Hou Fai wants the Macao SAR to also integrate the key projects included in other sections of the 15th Five-Year Plan, whenever it meets the necessary conditions to do so. Specifically, it required that six key points be clearly reflected in the 3rd Five-Year Plan, with the definition of specific tasks and the detailed implementation plan. First, he advocated intensifying cooperation with Mainland China in economy and trade, science and technology and humanistic exchanges, and improving policies and measures that facilitate Macau residents to develop and live in the Mainland. The other key points include advancing interconnection with the financial market in Mainland China and deepening the innovative collaboration of Industry-University-Research, and in addition, promoting the coordinated development of maritime terminals, airports and railway transport, with the construction of the Guangzhou-Zhuhai (Macau) fast train section as a priority project, including the connection line between the train station and the Hengqin border post. Furthermore, Sam Hou Fai urged improving the mechanisms that allow Macau to better play its role in opening the country to the outside world. He urged the Macao SAR to rigorously assume its role as an “infallible link” in cooperation between China and Portuguese-speaking and Spanish-speaking countries. On the other hand, he argued that Macau should take advantage of its fundamental role as a window for mutual exchange and learning between Chinese and Western civilizations. Secondly, Sam Hou Fai urged that the situation of Macau's internal and external development be analyzed in the next five years and the prospective vision and scientific rigor be reinforced in the pragmatic elaboration of the 3rd Five-Year Plan. As he highlighted, we must persist in the motto “based on the population” and give priority to issues of social well-being, as well as a clear orientation towards solving problems, with the aim of overcoming the obstacles that limit the adequate diversification of the economy and the improvement of the well-being of the population. In this sense, he argued that the 3rd Five-Year Plan should define the main indicators of economic and social development. Furthermore, Sam Hou Fai required that the preparation of each chapter have a clear division of tasks, making it necessary to specify the responsibilities and duties of the entity responsible for implementing it and the scope of necessary cooperation from the different guardianships. Furthermore, it considers that the Plan must include a distribution table of responsibilities relating to the main indicators, priority tasks and key projects, clearly defining the mechanisms for evaluating and supervising the implementation of the Plan.

item.title: Trump ignored allies, started the war and now asks them for ships in Hormuz
item.description:
The US President trusted his intuition and largely ignored diplomatic coordination when making the decision to launch attacks against Iran, with Israel. But now, with the economic and geopolitical consequences of the war unfolding rapidly, he is persuading allies and other global powers to help him solve the problem. Donald Trump says he has asked about half a dozen other countries to send warships to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, an important waterway through which a fifth of the world's oil trade passes. So far, none have committed. Trump even indicated that he would use his long-planned trip to China to pressure Beijing into helping a new coalition to restore oil tanker traffic through the strait – an idea that his Treasury secretary later downplayed. "We strongly encourage other nations whose economies depend on the strait much more than ours. We want them to come and help us with the strait," Trump said at the White House, citing Japan, China, South Korea and several European countries as examples. Trump has argued that the shipping canal is not something the US needs because of its own access to oil. It is this type of intimidation that won him important foreign policy victories in his second term, such as leading almost all NATO countries to increase defense spending in 2025, after allies were accused of taking advantage of American generosity, and using tariffs to extract investment and concessions from trading partners. China does not commit. Japan, Australia and European countries denied it, stressing that this war is not Europe's. Trump says the lack of interest in helping to secure the strait confirms his suspicions about the benefits of working with other countries, because "if we ever need help, they won't be there for us." “I always thought this was a weakness of NATO,” criticized Trump. “We were going to protect them, but I always said that when we needed them, they wouldn’t protect us.” Shortly afterwards, however, Trump insisted that the US does not need anyone's help because “we are the strongest nation in the world”. Despite this, the pressure continues. The White House press secretary, when asked why other nations that were not consulted or involved should put their troops in harm's way to ensure the safety of Hormuz, argued that other countries were directly benefiting from Trump's attempt to disarm the Iranian regime. “This is something that not just the US, but the entire Western world has agreed on for many, many years,” said Karoline Leavitt. On another occasion, Trump signaled to the Financial Times that “we would like to know”, before leaving for the summit in Beijing at the end of March, whether China will help guarantee the security of the strait due to its dependence on oil from the Middle East, adding: “We can postpone”. Hours later, at an event in the Oval Office, Trump revealed that he asked China to postpone the trip “for a month or more”. However, the cancellation of Chinese President Xi Jinping's visit could have major economic consequences, as relations between the world's two largest economies remain tense due to tariffs and other issues. In an interview with CNBC, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said any delay was not due to disputes over the strait and explicitly asked investors not to react negatively if Trump postpones the trip. “If the meeting is rescheduled for any reason, it will be for logistical reasons,” Bessent said in Paris, where he met with Chinese Vice Premier He Lifeng for a new round of trade negotiations. In the early days of the conflict with Iran, Trump claimed that US Navy ships would escort oil tankers through the strait and downplayed the threat posed by Iran. But, with oil prices skyrocketing, he and his government were forced to consider new options – including the idea, now advanced, that other countries join the initiative with their own warships. Bessent downplayed the impact of the war on oil prices and accused the media of “trying to turn this into a crisis that doesn’t exist”. Echoing Trump, the secretary insisted that prices would fall after the end of the conflict. China, facing its own economic pressures, recently lowered its 2026 growth target slightly to 4.5% to 5%, the slowest projected growth since 1991, meaning prolonged disruptions across the strait could have long-term impacts for Beijing as well. At the White House, Trump was asked what his advisers told him about how long gasoline prices will remain high. Trump ignored the question, showing once again that he ultimately trusts his own instincts. “I don’t need advisors to tell me that,” he said. “I know what it is.”
JTM with international agencies

item.title: Macao SAR as a scientific and technological “power”
item.description:
The improvement of the scientific and technological innovation system and the reinforcement of the transformation of scientific and technological results are some goals to be achieved by the Government, according to the Chief Executive. Speaking at the plenary meeting of the Science and Technology Council, Sam Hou Fai said that his government team will strive to “closely articulate the needs of the nation with the advantages of Macau”, in order to “become a scientific and technological power”. Together with the Deep Cooperation Zone in Hengqin, “Macau will consolidate and strengthen its development”, which will allow “to promote the adequate diversification of the economy and will support the creation, in the territory, of a hub of top-quality internationally qualified professionals”, he highlighted. Considering that the 15th Five-Year Plan period represents “a crucial five-year phase that serves as a bridge for the country to achieve the basic realization of socialist modernization”, Sam Hou Fai highlighted that it is also “essential” for Macau, “marking the transition from recovery-driven growth to high-quality development”. To this end, the Executive “will boost the high-quality development of the science and technology industry, bringing together collective wisdom and knowledge to jointly chart a strategic course that accelerates technological advancement”, he mentioned. At the same time, he pointed out that efforts will be made to complete the creation of the government guidance fund this year. “This fund will play a leading role in directing social capital to priority industries and key projects, thus robustly driving industrial modernization and the development of scientific innovation.” Furthermore, the words of the Head of Government pointed to the need to “effectively manage and promote international research teams and scientific organizations established in Macau, improve the internationalized scientific and technological services system and cultivate scientific and technological professionals with a global orientation”, even though this requires “greater reflection and additional efforts”. He highlighted, on the other hand, the need to face “deeply rooted” issues, such as insufficient support for new business models in emerging industries and their respective platforms, and the incomplete effect of the chain on “attracting, training, valuing and retaining” high-level qualified staff. Also “the lack of space for them to apply their talents and the uncertain impact of international strategic competition on scientific research and innovation” are factors to take into account. “Accelerating the correction of weaknesses in development and boosting technological innovation to boost industrial growth will require greater consensus and continued exploration”, he warned.
V.R.

item.title: Influenza virus at a high prevalence level
item.description:
The Health Services revealed that the flu virus has become more active recently, exceeding the alert threshold of 15.2% and generally having a high prevalence level. The health entity warns of reinforcing prevention measures
The flu virus in the territory has become more active recently, having surpassed the “alert threshold of 15.2%”, according to the Health Services (SSM), adding that the infectious agent is, in general, at a “high prevalence” level. Therefore, the SSM alert residents to reinforce prevention measures, especially pregnant women, children, the elderly and people with chronic illnesses who have not yet been vaccinated against the flu. “If infected, these groups are more likely to develop serious complications or even die, so they should be vaccinated as quickly as possible”, emphasizes the health authority. The influenza virus in Macau registered a peak between mid-November 2025 and the beginning of January 2026, a period in which subtype A (H3) mainly prevailed, having recently become more active again. According to laboratory test results, the positivity rate for the flu virus gradually rose from 5.0% in the previous three weeks (February 22nd to 28th) to 17.8% last week (March 8th to 14th), exceeding the alert level of 15.2%. More than 70% of cases were caused by type B virus. According to data released by the emergency services of three hospitals, there was a significant increase in the number of children seeking care due to flu-like illnesses. Since the end of February, this number has gradually increased, from 177 consultations in the previous three weeks to 342 consultations last week. On the other hand, the SSM statement highlights, the number of adult consultations for flu-like illnesses remained relatively stable. Regarding outbreaks of collective infection cases of flu-like illnesses, more cases were recorded in kindergartens and primary and secondary schools, mainly caused by the type B virus. “The number has been gradually increasing since mid-February, going from zero cases in the previous three weeks to 10 last week, with nine cases being registered in the first three days of this week”, emphasize the SSM, indicating that, since January until now, there have been no serious cases recorded. Furthermore, last Tuesday, four reports of cases of collective infection with flu-like illnesses were received.
V.R.

item.title: Macau has 126 traditional medicine ambassadors
item.description:
The MSAR's team of traditional Chinese medicine ambassadors already has 126 professionals, according to the director of Health Services, representing more than double the initial 47 members. Alvis Lo stated that the expansion “significantly” increased the “professionalism of the team”
Macao SAR currently has 126 traditional Chinese medicine (TCM) health promotion ambassadors, according to Alvis Lo, director of Health Services (SSM), which represents a growth of 168%, compared to November 2024, when the team was created, with 47 individuals. Currently, 30% of the professionals who make up the team have a master's degree and 13% have a doctorate. For the director, the arrival of the new ambassadors “significantly increased the size and professionalism of the team”. The data was presented by Alvis Lo, as part of the inaugural ceremony of the “Ambassadors for Health Promotion through Traditional Chinese Medicine of Macau” initiative, which brought together around 150 participants, including representatives from the Central Liaison Office in the Macao SAR and 29 local medical associations and institutions, as well as the new ambassadors, according to a statement from the SSM and the Institute for Pharmaceutical Supervision and Administration (ISAF). In 2023, SSM launched activities on the health culture of traditional Chinese medicine in schools. The following year, together with ISAF, they created the group of ambassadors, “with the aim of promoting the health culture of traditional Chinese medicine in the community, schools and companies”. The current team of professionals includes acupuncturists, TCM doctors and pharmacists. To date, more than 110 scientific dissemination lectures have been held, benefiting more than 11,000 residents and providing 120 traditional Chinese medicine health consultations with “remarkable results,” the statement said. Alvis Lo also said he hopes that the ambassadors “continue to penetrate the grassroots and get closer to residents” in the dissemination and promotion of traditional Chinese medicine. The SSM director described TCM as “a treasure of Chinese culture”. “In building a healthy city, traditional Chinese medicine provides important support for the physical and mental health of residents in the areas of nutrition, sport, emotional management and sleep”, he concluded. On the same occasion, Choi Peng Cheong, director of ISAF, stressed that the integration of TCM into daily life “can further improve the health and well-being of the population and promote the physical and mental health of the population”. Choi also asked the ambassadors to play their role in telling the stories of traditional Chinese medicine.

item.title: Tourism sector reinforces first aid techniques
item.description:
Reinforcing knowledge about first aid techniques and the awareness of workers in the tourism industry, with a view to providing a safe and peaceful environment and safeguarding the image of Macau as a tourist city, was the basis of a seminar on this subject. The main recipients were frontline professionals
The Directorate of Tourism Services (DST) and the Red Cross once again organized the “Seminar on First Aid”, the first of two sessions of which was attended by around 250 people. According to a note from DST, the initiative aims to continue to reinforce knowledge about first aid techniques and the awareness of industry workers, “with a view to providing a safe and peaceful environment and safeguarding the image as a tourist city, deepening the development of Macau as a global tourism and leisure center”. The main recipients of the first session were frontline tourism industry professionals, namely hotels, restaurants, retail sales, travel agencies and tour guides on duty. Based on the provision of quality services, the training serves to “reinforce confidence and the capacity to respond to life-threatening emergencies that workers from different sectors of the tourism industry encounter, through a generalization of education on first aid”, highlights the DST statement. The main speaker was Wong Ka Keong, a Red Cross counselor. The content of the training covered first aid principles, how to use an AED (Automatic External Defibrillator), what to do in case of shock, choking, drowning, traumatic injuries (including soft tissue injuries, hemorrhages, burns, among others), environmental traumas (such as heatstroke) and internal medicine emergencies (such as heart attack). DST plans to continue organizing courses, lectures and workshops to encourage and help tour operators to reinforce training, “raising the quality of tourist services in general and the effectiveness of their work”.
V.R.

item.title: “Human presence” in architecture motivates photography competition
item.description:
Until May 3rd, photographers of all levels can participate in the fifth CURB – Center for Architecture and Urbanism Architectural Photography Competition. This year’s theme, “People in Architecture”, invites the public to show “how architecture comes to life through human presence”. In addition to monetary prizes, the best photographs will be selected for an exhibition
Submissions for the fifth edition of the CURB – Center for Architecture and Urbanism Macau Architecture Photography Competition are open until May 3rd. “People in Architecture” is this year’s theme, under which the organization invites participants to “capture how architecture comes to life through human presence, highlighting the ways in which everyday interactions, movement and activity shape the experience of built environments”, can be read in a press release. The competition has two categories, one open to anyone interested and the other aimed at students. In the general category, the prizes are three thousand, two thousand and one thousand patacas for the first three classified. Among students, prizes vary between 1,500, 1,000 and 500 patacas. Submissions will be evaluated by an experienced jury, with the best photographs being selected for an exhibition. Photographs can be uploaded to the competition page of the CURB website. Architecture “is more than a form or structure”, describes CURB. “It gains meaning through the people who interact with it.” "Designed to welcome life, meetings, and moments of reflection, the spaces come to life through daily interactions. Through photography, the competition invites participants to reveal how these human dimensions give character and vitality to architecture", he adds. The competition has been taking place since 2022, having attracted enthusiasts and photographers of all levels, from amateurs and students to professionals. With “exceptional results and growing interest”, over the four years, the competition saw the participation of 688 people and 1,611 submissions. CURB aims to promote research, education, production and dissemination of knowledge in fields such as architecture, urbanism or urban culture. It operates at a regional level, between Macau, the Greater Bay Area and Mainland China, and abroad, mainly in Portuguese-speaking and Asian countries. The Center positions itself as a platform for exchange between academia, society, professionals and government institutions, serving the interest of the community, through research, workshops, conferences, exhibitions and competitions, among other initiatives.

item.title: Young people interested in vacancies at technology companies
item.description: The Department of Economic and Technological Development Services (DSEDT) and the Department of Labor Affairs Services (DSAL) organized a meeting session with technology companies and Macau residents and young people who wish to dedicate themselves to the technology sector. The event had 220 registrations, with a total of 198 participations in the interviews, of which around 80% were young people aged 34 or younger. Candidates who pass the second interview stage will be contacted directly by the companies, and the results of the selection process will be announced by the end of April. As for the participating companies, they included firms certified under the DSEDT “Technological Companies Certification Program”, but also two from the Deep Cooperation Zone in Hengqin and 24 from Macau. The areas of activity cover artificial intelligence, integrated circuits, software development and network engineering, among other technological aspects, offering more than 130 job vacancies, of which around 20 are internships. The vacancies include data and artificial intelligence strategy consultants, chip validation engineers, data development engineers, systems and software maintenance engineers, “providing diversified career options for Macau residents and young people looking for work”, according to a joint statement from DSEDT and DSAL, reiterating that “one of the Government's priorities is to “promote the development of the technology industry and train qualified local staff”.

item.title: Poetry session invites you to reflect on the “tree”
item.description: “A Poetry Meeting: The Tree and everything you want” is a session scheduled for today afternoon (6:30 pm), at the Rui Cunha Foundation, to celebrate World Poetry Day, a date established by UNESCO at the 30th General Conference in Paris, in 1999, but also the International Day of Forests, both marked on March 21st by the United Nations. The session, which will be led by doctor and writer Shee Vá, will invite the public to share poems with special meaning in their lives, on the theme of the “Tree” or not, says the press release, “honoring poetic expression and its cultural importance as an identity legacy of the language of each country or countries”. Co-organized by the Reading Club of the Associação dos Amigos do Livro in Macau, the event is an opportunity to encourage reading, support poets and preserve linguistic and cultural diversity, through the written and spoken word, says the organization. “Poetry, with its ability to move, provoke reflection and transmit feelings, has been able to cross centuries and civilizations, being one of the pillars of literary art”, highlights the FRC, inviting the public to appear at the Foundation Gallery and ask to speak. “Come prepared to listen and read, contributing to enriching the experience of local communities in this aim of giving voice to the feelings of poets and touching souls”, he adds. The event, which will be held in Portuguese, is free to enter.

item.title: MACAU URGED TO KEEP OPEN TO FOREIGN STAFF
item.description: The commissioner of the Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs in Macau stressed yesterday that, as an international center for cooperation, the city must remain “open to foreign talent”. In a session on the country's 15th Five-Year Plan and its impact on Macau's development, Bian Lixin highlighted that “visa facilitation and cross-border mobility measures will be strengthened to attract highly qualified professionals”. These include preferential actions that facilitate the cross-border movement of foreigners between Macau and Hengqin and visa facilitation policies for foreigners with permanent residence in Macau who travel to the Mainland, supporting the construction of the Macao SAR as an “international center for high-level talent”. In July 2023, Macau created a program to attract qualified professionals from the financial sector and the areas of scientific and technological research and development, including Nobel Prize holders. In October, the secretary-general of the Talent Development Commission said that Macau approved 464 of the more than a thousand applications for the program. The overwhelming majority of staff are from Mainland China (80%) and Hong Kong (10%), with 47% having “experience of working or studying abroad”. On the other hand, residence in Macau for Portuguese people has become more restricted since August 2023, and they are now treated like other foreigners, focusing on family reunification or very specific technical skills. According to Bian, his office will align diplomatic efforts to ensure that the local development strategy is articulated with national priorities. He also highlighted that Macau must “strengthen its diplomacy”, taking advantage of ties with Portuguese and Spanish-speaking countries to deepen cooperation within the scope of the “One Belt, One Road” initiative and expand exchanges with Eurasia and Southeast Asia.

item.title: Chinese cliff village attracts visitors with cable car
item.description:
Situated on a steep cliff 1,400 meters above sea level and overlooking a river gorge in southwestern Sichuan province, the village of Gulu has always faced development challenges due to its inaccessibility. The village is home to 645 people, around 80% of whom belong to the Yi ethnic group. Recently, there has been a tourism boom, along with improvements in basic infrastructure. In 2018, not only was a 750-meter-long cable car line built to improve community connectivity, but electricity and 5G networks were also installed. The cable car has become a major attraction for visitors. Family accommodations have emerged as hikers flock to the village, attracted by the stunning views of the Dadu River Canyon National Geological Park, considered one of the most beautiful in the country. Other villages that have become popular tourist destinations due to their natural landscapes include Shibadong Village in the Wuling Mountains of Hunan Province and Huangling in Jiangxi Province, known for its picturesque agricultural terraces. Gulu village's annual per capita income grew from less than 2,000 yuan before 2014, to more than 21,000 in 2025. About 300,000 visits were made to the village in 2025, generating total revenue of 12 million yuan, according to Zheng Wangchun, party secretary of the village. No wonder, then, that Gulu was hailed as a successful example of China's rural revitalization efforts in the country's recent “Two Sessions.” Zheng, who is also a deputy of the National People's Congress (ANP), highlighted to a Singapore newspaper that what the general secretary (Xi Jinping) said was true: “on the path to poverty alleviation, we will not leave any ethnic group or individual behind”. Zheng himself is among the businesspeople who returned to the village. He worked in Guangdong province for a few years, and in 2025, he managed a guesthouse in the village, with an annual income of around 200,000 yuan. Zheng, deputy to the ANP since 2023, defended the creation of additional infrastructure for tourists, such as 5G base stations, parking lots and viewpoints. It is not yet known whether Gulu's model can be replicated in other remote rural communities. China has about 484,000 villages and a total rural population of about 451 million. Chen Bo, a researcher who studies China's economy at the Institute of East Asian Studies in Singapore, said Gulu's case may be an exception, but if other villages have beautiful natural landscapes, attracting tourists could be a viable strategy. “But for many remote villages in mountainous areas, building cable cars and highways may not be a good idea, as construction and maintenance can be very expensive,” he said. Instead, moving rural residents to urban areas may be more effective. In fact, the government has eased restrictions to make life more convenient for rural residents in urban areas, where the social security system is more robust, he added. Although China is advancing in high-tech sectors such as artificial intelligence and robotics, it still faces significant challenges in improving the quality of life of the rural population. At the end of China's last Five-Year Plan, in 2025, the government stated that the income gap between urban and rural areas had narrowed to 2.31 to 1, compared to 2.56 to 1 in 2020, however, the difference remains large. For example, Gulu's per capita income is still less than half of Sichuan's urban level, which is around 49,000 yuan. Ensuring that Gulu's popularity is not fleeting is one of Zheng's concerns. In previous interviews, he acknowledged that Gulu's tourism industry is still in its infancy and that, in this regard, it is necessary to learn from other villages. China declared victory over absolute poverty in rural areas in 2021, marking the official end of a decades-long campaign that lifted more than 700 million people from the poverty line, defined as an annual income of about 4,000 yuan in 2020 prices. But economists have pointed to the need to close the gap in social services received by rural and urban residents, for example, to ensure long-term returns in fighting poverty. At a strategic level, China has turned to greater investment in human capital. He also announced measures to train farmers in high-tech equipment and entrepreneurship, in addition to just granting subsidies. The 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-2030) outlines a shift in emphasis from building physical infrastructure to development, a framework geared towards greater investment in child care, elderly care, health and education for all. Whether or not this bears fruit will have implications not only for the country's innovation and growth, but also for tangible results for a vast rural population that the country has committed to not leaving behind.
JTM with international agencies
